channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Here's how a robot massage compares to the real thing
item.description: First, you book an appointment through an app - it's sort of like getting an Uber. You pick a time and place, and it's all confirmed on the spot. A half-hour massage is $60 at the location I visited.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KRON4

item.title: Olivia Hussey, star of the 1968 film 'Romeo and Juliet,' dies at 73
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Olivia Hussey, the actor who starred as a teenage Juliet in the 1968 film “Romeo and Juliet,” has died, her family said on social media Saturday. She was 73. Hussey died on Friday, “peacefully at home surrounded by her loved ones,” a statement posted to her Instagram account said.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Abortions are up in the US. It's a complicated picture as women turn to pills, travel
item.description: Even with abortion bans in place in most Republican-controlled states, the number of people obtaining them has grown slightly.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kevin S. Vineys, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NY correctional officers repeatedly struck handcuffed inmate, picked him up by his neck before he died: video
item.description: Correctional officers at a New York prison repeatedly punched a handcuffed inmate and lifted him by the neck before dropping him, newly released video shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TN announces new execution method that could allow state to resume executions after last one halted in 2022
item.description: Tennessee announced Friday a new method that could allow the state to resume executions after the last scheduled execution was abruptly halted in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Winning $1.22 billion Mega Millions ticket sold in California
item.description: One lucky Californian is the state's newest billionaire after winning numbers for the $1.22 billion Mega Millions jackpot were drawn Friday night. The winning numbers were 3, 7, 37, 49, 55 and the Mega Ball number was 6. The Megaplier was 3X.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KTLA 5

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: New Mega Millions jackpot stands at $1.22B after no winner during Christmas Eve drawing
item.description: The Mega Millions jackpot continues to grow after no ticket matched all six numbers drawn on Christmas Eve. The new estimated jackpot for Friday night’s drawing is $1.22 billion, California Lottery officials announced in a post on X. This is the fifth largest jackpot in the game's history.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Manchin objects to Biden's commutations
item.description: Outgoing Sen. Joe Manchin (I-W.Va.) is denouncing as “misguided” and “disgusting” President Biden's move to commute the sentences of two men convicted of murdering a student at Marshall University.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: US homelessness up 18% as affordable housing remains out of reach for many people
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The United States saw an 18.1% increase in homelessness this year, a dramatic rise driven mostly by a lack of affordable housing as well as devastating natural disasters and a surge of migrants in several parts of the country, federal officials said Friday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: In states that ban abortion, social safety net programs often fail families
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Taylor Cagnacci moved from California to Tennessee with hopes of starting a new chapter in a state that touts a low cost of living and natural beauty.
item.dc:creator: LAURA UNGAR and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico tests app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: U.S. authorities are obliged to give notice to home-country consulates when a foreign citizen is detained. Mexico says it has beefed up consular staff and legal aid to help migrants in the legal process related to deportation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Lava erupts from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Onion-wielding man disrupts Christmas church services, pours whiskey into holy water: deputies
item.description: A man was arrested and charged after disrupting religious services at two Maryland Catholic churches on Christmas Eve, according to the St. Mary's County Sherriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Musk stirs pot on X over 'shortage' of talented American engineers
item.description: Musk, an immigrant from South Africa, posted to X that there is a shortage of "talented" engineers in the U.S. and companies should start looking to other countries.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Most blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death: poll
item.description: Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What is known about the Kazakhstan plane crash that killed 38 of the 67 on board
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner has crashed in Kazakhstan after being diverted, killing 38 of 67 people on board.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Richard Perry, record producer behind 'You're So Vain' and other hits, dies at 82
item.description: Richard Perry, a hitmaking record producer with a flair for both standards and contemporary sounds whose many successes included Carly Simon’s “You’re So Vain,” Rod Stewart’s “The Great American Songbook” series and a Ringo Starr album featuring all four Beatles, died Tuesday. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Seafarers move global trade
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — On a balmy December evening, four Indian crew members, eager to go explore this Mediterranean city where their container ship docked for a few hours, turned for help to Stella Maris.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL’ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 people rescued when California pier partially collapses
item.description: A portion of the Santa Cruz Wharf has collapsed and is floating away Monday, according to the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart illegally opened bank accounts for 1 million drivers, lawsuit alleges
item.description: Walmart and fintech company Branch Messenger are being sued by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for allegedly forcing more than one million delivery workers to use expensive deposit accounts to access their paychecks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Officer fatally shot in a North Carolina supermarket, suspect in custody, police say
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (AP) — A police officer responding to a report of a man with a gun inside a North Carolina supermarket was fatally shot Monday and a suspect was later taken into custody, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inside the state and federal charges against the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A triptych of criminal charges paints a searing, sometimes disparate portrait of the man accused of ambushing and killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO Brian Thompson as the executive arrived at a Manhattan hotel for his company’s annual investor conference.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Third entangled endangered whale spotted in span of a week off East Coast
item.description: A third endangered whale has been spotted entangled in fishing gear off the East Coast, marking an alarming end to the year for a species threatened with extinction.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione pleads not guilty to state charges in death of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare pleaded not guilty on Monday to murder and terror charges in a state case that will run parallel to his federal prosecution.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York City police apprehend suspect in the death of a woman found on fire in a subway car
item.description: New York City police announced Sunday they have in custody a “person of interest” in the early morning death of a woman who they believe may have fallen asleep on a stationary subway train before being intentionally lit on fire by a man she didn't know.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missouri company creates healthy dog treats using invasive fish
item.description: A Missouri company has found a way to turn an invasive fish into multiple useful products, including a new line of dog treats.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, WGN 9
